headline: Agent work needs proof, not vibes
source_notes: Demand is Jobs-to-be-Done; lanes are fulfillment.
Surfaces are projections; the ledger owns truth.
OpenClaw can converse, while JobLane gates durable effects.
x_posts: Agent systems become easier to trust when every workflow has one orchestrator of record.
A good lane pack should travel; state, secrets, and surfaces should stay home.
The difference between an agent demo and an agent product is the ledger.
review_questions: Is the argument specific enough for a public audience?
Does the packet leak implementation details that should stay private?
